question: How does the NuScale secondary side interface with the primary system through the steam generator?

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the main Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and its connections. The section also briefly introduces the pressurizer (PZR), which is integral to the RPV.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including low alloy steel for the baffle plate, stainless steel and nickel-based alloys for cladding and components, all in accordance with relevant BPVC specifications. It also references where further material descriptions can be found in other report sections and lists the applicable standards for each material.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, with the RPV itself forming the shell and pressure boundary for the SGs, ensuring separation between the primary and secondary sides of the system.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across different power levels by establishing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures, using both software and manual calculations. It accounts for uncertainties in flow resistance and thermal head, ensuring efficient operation, safety margins, and reliable natural circulation with minimal pressure losses in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module's Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details how the steam generators are integrated into the reactor vessel, forming part of the primary and secondary system boundaries, and references other sections for further information on specific components.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design removes noncondensible gases from the pressurizer (PZR) during both normal and emergency operations, helping maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary. The RCS, located inside the containment vessel, circulates coolant by natural convection to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generators, with its components and interfaces described and illustrated in accompanying figures.

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat during non-LOCA events when normal cooling is unavailable, ensuring the reactor transitions safely to shutdown without external power. The system maintains the reactor coolant system (RCS) temperature below 420°F within 36 hours, protecting fuel and pressure boundaries, and its reliability and risk significance are evaluated through established programs. The DHRS operates independently of the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS), but both can function together if needed.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It details how actuation valves are exercised and tested, including during cold shutdown conditions, and outlines the initial in-situ test to verify the DHRS’s ability to remove heat from the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the first installed module.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards related to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, inspection, and quality assurance.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer in the NuScale reactor is integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and functions to regulate reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing continuous monitoring and control.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure boundary to prevent the release of fission products and provides structural support for various reactor components, with all materials and fabrication processes meeting stringent ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code requirements to ensure long-term integrity and safety.

edges.description: Section A describes operational steps involving the pressurizer and system isolation in the NuScale design, while Section B provides detailed material specifications for the pressurizer components referenced in Section A's operational context.

Section A describes operational procedures involving the steam generators (SGs) and their isolation from the main steam and feedwater systems, while Section B provides the design context by specifying that the SGs are integral components within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) that interface between the primary and secondary systems.

Section A describes operational steps involving the steam generators (SGs) and their isolation from main systems, while Section B evaluates the SGs' role in transferring heat from the primary to the secondary side via the DHRS, connecting the operational interface described in A to the functional evaluation in B.

Section A describes operational procedures involving the steam generators (SGs) and their isolation from the main steam and feedwater systems, while Section B provides the design context by identifying the SGs as integral components within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) that interface between the primary and secondary systems.

Section B describes the overall configuration of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, explicitly noting the inclusion of two steam generators that interface between the primary and secondary systems, while Section A focuses on the pressurizer components and their materials, both as subsystems within the same RPV design.

Section B provides a system-level evaluation of how the steam generator (SG) interfaces with both the primary and secondary systems, while Section A references detailed component and material descriptions (including cross-references to other sections) that underpin the design and function of the primary system components involved in this interface.
Section A provides detailed material specifications for pressurizer components that are generally described as part of the reactor pressure vessel and its subsystems in Section B.

Section A describes the physical components and configuration of the NuScale steam generators within the reactor coolant system, while Section B discusses how the steam generators functionally interface with the secondary side to remove decay heat from the primary coolant, thus connecting the design details in A to the operational evaluation in B.

Section A discusses the operational evaluation and performance criteria of the NuScale system, referencing the steam generator's role in decay heat removal, while Section B provides the detailed design and configuration of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators that interface between the primary and secondary systems.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system (RCS) and its components, while Section B specifies operational details and procedures involving the steam generators (SGs) and their interface with secondary systems, thus Section B provides details for Section A's general description.
Section B provides detailed material specifications for pressurizer components that are generally described as part of the reactor coolant system in Section A.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor coolant system (RCS), which includes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B specifies that the RPV contains two steam generators that serve as the interface between the primary (RCS) and secondary systems.

Section A describes the function of the reactor coolant system (RCS) degasification line and reactor vent valves (RVVs) in removing noncondensible gases from the pressurizer (PZR), while Section B details the operational steps involving the steam generators (SGs), including their isolation and the use of the degasification line to vent nitrogen from the PZR, thus connecting the primary and secondary systems through shared operational procedures.

Section A provides a general system-level description of the reactor coolant system (RCS) and its degasification and pressure boundary functions, while Section B specifies detailed material and component information for the pressurizer, which is a subsystem within the RCS described in Section A.

Section A describes the overall reactor coolant system (RCS) and its components, while Section B specifies that the steam generators are integrated within the RCS and details their role as part of the interface between the primary (RCS) and secondary systems.

Section A describes the function of the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) in removing heat via the secondary side when normal cooling is unavailable, while Section B details the operational steps involving the steam generators (SGs) and their isolation from the main steam and feedwater systems, thus providing specific procedural context for how the secondary side interfaces with the primary system through the SGs during DHRS operation.

Section A describes the Decay Heat Removal System's reliance on secondary-side cooling, which is facilitated by the steam generators detailed in Section B as integral components connecting the primary reactor coolant system to the secondary side.
Section B provides specific details about the reactor coolant system's components and functions, elaborating on the general system described in Section A.

Section A describes the primary system's design and its reliance on natural circulation, which is the basis for how the secondary side (including systems like the DHRS in Section B) interfaces with and removes heat from the primary system through the steam generator.

Section A describes the reactor coolant system (RCS) and its pressure boundary, which is the primary system interfaced by the secondary side via the steam generator, while Section B details the Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) that relies on this interface to remove heat from the RCS when normal secondary-side cooling is unavailable.

Section A details inspection and classification requirements for DHRS components including steam piping and valves associated with the steam generator, while Section B describes operational procedures involving the steam generator's isolation and interface with primary system components such as the pressurizer and containment, thus both sections are connected through their focus on the steam generator's role and associated components at the primary-secondary interface.

Section A discusses inspection and classification requirements for components including the steam piping and valves associated with the steam generator, while Section B describes the physical integration of the steam generators within the reactor pressure vessel, connecting the secondary side to the primary system; thus, Section A provides operational and regulatory details for components whose physical arrangement is described in Section B.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the integrity and quality assurance of the reactor coolant boundary, which includes the steam generator interface described in Section B's summary of system operations and component interactions.

Section B references Section A by citing Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, establishing a direct cross-reference for component material information relevant to the interface between the secondary and primary systems.

Section A provides references to regulatory and technical standards that underpin the design and integrity requirements for the reactor coolant boundary, which includes the steam generator interface described in Section B as part of the reactor coolant system component design.

Section A describes the pressurizer's function and its integration with the reactor vessel, while Section B discusses operational procedures involving the pressurizer and steam generators, connecting them through the management of pressure and water levels during system transitions.

Section A describes the functional design and role of the pressurizer within the reactor vessel, while Section B provides references to detailed material specifications and component descriptions that support and elaborate on Section A's general overview.

Section B describes the overall configuration of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, explicitly stating that both the pressurizer (from Section A) and the steam generators are integrated components, thereby establishing their physical and functional proximity within the primary system.

Section A discusses inspection and classification requirements for components including the steam piping and valves associated with the steam generator, while Section B lists references such as ASME and EPRI guidelines that provide the technical standards and quality assurance requirements those components must meet, thus connecting Section A's implementation details to Section B's referenced standards.

Section A describes the physical integration of the steam generators within the reactor vessel as part of the NuScale Power Module, while Section B discusses the operational procedures involving the steam generators' connection to the main steam and feedwater systems, thus both sections are connected through their focus on the steam generators as the interface between the primary and secondary systems.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor vessel and its components, including the steam generators, while Section B specifies detailed material and design information for the pressurizer, which is part of the reactor vessel described in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor vessel and its components, including the steam generators, while Section B specifies the detailed design and internal arrangement of those components, particularly the steam generators, within the reactor vessel.
